title: House GOP on Trump’s 2027 pay freeze: ‘That’s politics’
summary: Republicans on the House Appropriations Committee last week beat back multiple attempts to increase federal workers’ pay next year and restore their workplace rights.

title: Former civil servants aim to shape policy as members of Congress
summary: Dozens of federal employees who left or were pushed out of government in 2025 are now running for office.

title: Agencies doled out $186B in improper payments last year, GAO says
summary: That fiscal year 2025 improper payment number is up by $24 billion from the previous fiscal year, even as the Trump administration says that it’s tamping down on fraud.

title: From bowling repairs to zoology, Trump admin consolidates job titles affecting 5,000 feds
summary: The impacted employees will not lose their jobs and OPM says it will help them be more agile.

title: The ‘doers’ need a budget: Why a $100 million council fund can end federal management failures
summary: COMMENTARY | The CFO, CIO, and CHCO councils are full of "doers" who have the knowledge to fix pervasive government failures. Congress must grant them a direct, unrestricted $100 million budget, bypassing OMB, to finally test and scale solutions across federal silos.

title: Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents’ Dinner after shots fired
summary: Security forces responded to an incident near a screening area, and authorities took one person into custody.

title: Senate advances GOP budget blueprint to boost ICE and Border Patrol funding
summary: The measure cleared 50-48 after a marathon amendment session and sets up a reconciliation path for immigration enforcement spending.

title: NIST is giving fingerprint examiners better tools for a messy job
summary: COMMENTARY | A newly annotated fingerprint dataset combined with open-source software could help forensic examiners work more consistently, train more effectively and sort through evidence faster.

title: Fed employee appeals system independence at stake in new Supreme Court brief
summary: The high court has heard arguments in a similar case regarding the president’s authority to remove members of quasi-judicial agencies.

title: Catch me if you can’t: How fraudsters are outpacing the government
summary: COMMENTARY | The government must stop chasing money lost to fraud and instead focus on preventing the crime before payments go out. This requires policy changes so agencies can share information and be rewarded for stopping losses at the source.

title: Executive order on mail ballots tests limits of Postal Service independence
summary: Postal experts and former officials say the directive could reshape longstanding boundaries between the White House and the Postal Service and is already facing legal challenges over its authority and impact on election administration.

title: USDA kicks off more employee relocations, including some that spark déjà vu
summary: Hundreds of employees will be reassigned to Iowa, Missouri, Colorado and elsewhere.

title: IRS lacks transparent plans to leverage tech in the face of staffing cuts, GAO and employees say
summary: Agency leaders are “shoving AI at us,” one IRS employee said, despite the fact that “they don’t have the right tools for us yet.”

title: They did everything right and still haven’t been paid
summary: Months after retiring, some federal workers are stuck in a bureaucratic maze with no checks, no answers and no clear end in sight.

title: Experts say Trump inflated his deregulation numbers, but his process changes are here to stay
summary: The president has made several changes to the rulemaking process, including added White House scrutiny of certain regulations and exceptions to some time-consuming steps.

title: U.S. citizens shot by ICE urge Congress to rein in federal immigration agents
summary: Democrats highlight cases of U.S. citizens harmed in immigration enforcement actions during a House hearing that Republicans largely boycotted and key Trump officials skipped.

title: DHS to again stop paying employees in May if shutdown continues
summary: Republicans took a new step on Tuesday to end the 67-day standoff, but there is still no immediate end in sight.

title: Space Force scrambles to repair workforce as massive budget increase looms
summary: The service is trying to recruit at a record pace even as Pentagon officials insist civilian departures didn't hurt acquisition.

title: Transportation touts air traffic control modernization, presses Congress for more funding
summary: After decades of stalled efforts, DOT says its scaled-back modernization is on track, but needs billions more to finish, as lawmakers weigh the price and past failures.

title: OMB seeks details from agencies on their commercial buying, or lack thereof
summary: A new White House budget office memo also outlines what agencies have to do if they want to go down the non-commercial contracting route and who has the approval power over it.

title: RFK: Cuts at HHS haven’t led to problems, but we’re hiring 12,000 new employees
summary: "Nobody in the agency wants to cut these programs," Kennedy said, suggesting instead they were required by the White House.

title: OPM proposes hazard pay for more federal firefighting activities
summary: Under recently unveiled regulations, federal wildland firefighters would be eligible for a 25% increase in pay when they work on prescribed burns, a proactive tool to mitigate the risk of wildfires.

title: Contractors sue to block Trump’s federal DEI executive order
summary: The lawsuit alleges minority-owned businesses are being forced to trade their First Amendment rights for federal contracts.

title: When does federal service really end? DOD case challenges retirees’ speech rights
summary: A Pentagon effort to discipline retired Navy officer and Sen. Mark Kelly, D-Ariz., over public comments is raising broader questions about whether military retirees remain subject to federal authority, and what that could mean for speech rights, retirement benefits and post-service obligations.
